title: polystichum braunii var. andersonii
intro: Polystichum braunii var. andersonii, also known as Anderson's Black Fern, is a rare and endangered subspecies of the Polystichum braunii fern native to the southeastern United States. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Anderson's Black Fern include its ability to soothe coughs, reduce inflammation, and ease congestion, making it a valuable natural remedy for respiratory health. Herbal preparations of Polystichum braunii var. andersonii typically involve drying and infusing the fronds in hot water to make a tea, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
